expectations.text: has_all_sections: Spec has all required sections including Acceptance Criteria
expectations.evidence: All 9 required sections present: ## Problem (L13), ## Solution (L17), ## Scope (L21), ## Acceptance Criteria (L42), ## Design Decisions (L53), ### Failure Modes (L67), ## Risks (L74), ## Alternatives (L80), ## Verification (L86).

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Zero triple-backtick (```) occurrences in the entire spec. Inline backticks used for commands in Verification (L97-99) are not code fences.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: No TypeScript types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L53-72) contains no 'interface ', no type annotations (': string', ': number', ': boolean'), no '=> Promise'. The word 'type' on L55 ('title, type, position') is plain English describing panel attributes, not the TS keyword.

expectations.text: no_json_shapes: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains no JSON-like object literals, no data structure definitions, no schema shapes. All data descriptions are in prose form.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope (L25-32) contains zero file paths, zero 'src/' references, zero file extensions. All 8 items describe behavioral capabilities (e.g., 'Navigation tools: switch between dashboard tabs').

expectations.text: spec_under_120_lines: Under 120 lines excluding Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Implementation Delta starts at L118. Lines 1-117 = 117 lines, under the 120-line limit.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: 6/6 Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold declarative sentences: 'Dashboard context includes panel metric summaries...' (L55), 'Tools are split into read-only navigation...' (L57), 'Tab navigation uses the existing hash-based routing...' (L59), 'Panel identification in tools uses both...' (L61), 'Template variable changes flow through...' (L63), 'Context and tools hooks follow unconditional-call ordering...' (L65). 100% > 80%.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Implementer can start without questions
expectations.evidence: Spec names the three-step integration pattern (context hook, tools hook, chat store registration), state management approach (existing useState + hash routing), tool categorization (navigation=immediate, mutation=confirmed), panel identification strategy (title fuzzy-match + ID), context budget (4KB with truncation policy), and failure behaviors. Sufficient for a competent developer to begin.

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains React hook implementation details: L63 names specific hooks ('Variables live in Dashboard component's local useState, and changes propagate through useMetricQueries'), L65 prescribes a specific hook for a technical problem ('wraps all handler references in useLatestRef to prevent stale closures'), L65 names specific functions ('setContextGetter/clearContextGetter pattern'). These prescribe HOW to implement at the React hook level, not WHAT decision was made. Borderline=FAIL per grading rules.

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: Failure modes are policy decisions
expectations.evidence: 4/4 failure modes are genuine policy decisions: (1) zero fuzzy-match results → error, no partial-match fallback (team could argue for partial matching), (2) unknown variable → error listing valid names (could silently ignore), (3) save failure → no auto-retry (could auto-retry), (4) context >4KB → truncate least-recently-interacted panels first (could truncate by size, by position, etc.). 100% > 50%.

8/8 In Scope items are behavioral capabilities: chat context serialization, navigation tools, view manipulation tools, template variable tools, edit-mode tools, export tool, suggested prompts, confirmation flow. Zero file names or component references. 100% > 80%.

## Acceptance Criteria exists at L42 with 8 checkbox items (within 4-8 range). All items are observable outcomes: FAB visibility (L44), question answering accuracy (L45), tab switching with URL update (L46), panel fullscreen activation (L47), template variable re-evaluation (L48), confirmation card display (L49), valid JSON export (L50), graceful error handling (L51). None are implementation tasks.

### Agent-Verifiable subsection exists at L103 with 7 items in action → outcome format: 'Open Dashboard page → chat FAB is visible' (L105), 'Navigate away → FAB disappears' (L106), 'Send message → tab changes' (L107), 'Send message → panel enters fullscreen' (L108), 'Send message → confirmation card appears' (L109), 'Inspect context getter → summaries present' (L110), 'Inspect suggestedPromptsByPage → dashboard key exists' (L111). Human-Only section is separate and labeled optional.

expectations.text: has_appetite: Spec has an Appetite field in the header
expectations.evidence: L5: '**Appetite:** Small Batch (~1-2 weeks)' — field present with a real time-budget value, not a placeholder.

expectations.text: has_boundary_tiers: Design Decisions use three-tier boundaries where appropriate
expectations.evidence: 5 distinct tier statements across all 3 tiers: Always — 'keep serialized context under 4KB' (L55), 'set confirmationRequired on tools that persist data' (L57). Ask First — 'if a new tool's destructiveness is ambiguous, discuss before defaulting' (L57). Never — 'include raw time-series arrays or query definitions in context' (L55), 'allow a tool handler to throw' (L57). Well exceeds the 2-statement minimum.
